title: General Best Practices for Immunization | Vaccines & Immunizations | CDC
text:
Injury Compensation Program.
Preventing and Managing Adverse Reactions 
Review information for preventing and managing adverse reactions from vaccines. 
Vaccine Administration 
Discusses infection control and sterile technique, route of administration, multiple and jet injections. alleviating discomfort and pain, and clinical implications of nonstandard practices.
Vaccine Administration 
Read the general best practice guidelines for vaccine administration. 
Storage and Handling of Immunobiologics 
General principles such as storage temperature and response to out-of-range temperature reading.
Storage and Handling of Immunobiologics 
Read the general best practice guidelines for the storage and handling of immunobiologics. 
Altered Immunocompetence 
This section describes situations in which vaccines are recommended outside of the routine-age-based recommendation because the risk for vaccine-preventable disease is increased due to altered immunocompetence.
Altered Immunocompetence 
Read the general best recommendations and protocols for individuals with compromised immune systems... 
Special Situations 
Describes several special situations in immunization, including concurrent administration with antimicrobial agents, vaccination outside the United States, administration of live vaccines and Tuberculin Skin Tests (TSTs) and Interferon-gamma Release Assays (IGRAs), and vaccinati
